headline: Sabah Filipino-Muslim Community: 180,000 Citizens Without Full Social Acceptance
context: An estimated 180,000 Filipino-Muslim Malaysians — citizens by birth or naturalisation — report persistent discrimination in employment, housing, and social services in Sabah.

cards.sub: Despite legal status, 62% report discrimination in job applications when their heritage is identified.

cards.big: Employer surveys show 34% would not hire Filipino-heritage Malaysians.
cards.sub: The discrimination is based on ancestry, not citizenship — legally protected under Article 8.

cards.big: Filipino-Muslim communities in Sabah have poverty rates of 28%.
cards.sub: This is 5 times the Sabah average and comparable to Orang Asli poverty in the Peninsula.

cards.big: Project IC controversy created lasting stigma against all Filipino-heritage Malaysians.
cards.sub: Legitimate citizens bear the social cost of political manipulation they did not participate in.

cards.big: Citizens who are treated as outsiders because of their parents' origin. Citizenship promises equality. Sabah's Filipino-Muslims have the paper but not the practice.
Discrimination based on ancestry among citizens is unconstitutional. Addressing it requires anti-discrimination legislation and public education — not just legal status.
